data.title: Healthcare Realty declares $0.31 dividend

• Healthcare Realty (NYSE:HR) declares $0.31/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 7.3%
  • Payable March 19; for shareholders of record March 3; ex-div March 3.
  • See HR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Gildan Activewear beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Gildan Activewear press release (NYSE:GIL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $822M (+5.0% Y/Y) beats by $16.25M.
  • Cash flow from operations of $211 million in Q4 and $501 million for the full year; free cash flow of $208 million in Q4 and $389 million for the full year.
  • Capital returned to shareholders of $246 million in Q4 and $889 million for the full year, through dividends and share repurchases.
  • Company announces 10% dividend increase for 2025.
  • Company initiates 2025 annual guidance.

  • For 2025, we expect the following:

    • Revenue growth for the full year to be up mid-single digits vs. estimated growth of 4.55% Y/Y;
    • Full year adjusted operating margin1 to be up approximately 50 basis points;
    • Capex to come in at approximately 5% of sales;
    • Adjusted diluted EPS1 in the range of $3.38 to $3.58, up between ~13% and ~19% year over year vs. $3.48 consensus;
    • Free cash flow to be above $450 million.
    • For the first quarter of 2025, net sales are expected to be up low single digits year over year. Excluding the impact of the Under Armour sock license agreement, Q1 net sales are expected to be up mid-single digits. Adjusted operating margin1 is expected to be in line with our full year guidance of 'up approximately 50 basis points'. Of note, the enactment of GMT in Canada and Barbados only took place during the second quarter of 2024. As such, on a comparable basis, the Company's adjusted effective income tax rate1 in the first quarter of 2025 is expected to be significantly higher than the 3.6% recorded in the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: Materion Non-GAAP EPS of $1.55 beats by $0.10, revenue of $436.9M beats by $3.68M

data.title: Fiverr International rallies after issuing strong profit guidance
data.description:
Fiverr International (NYSE:FVRR) jumped in early trading on Wednesday after the Israel-based company topped revenue estimates with its Q4 earnings report and issued promising guidance.

Revenue was up 13.3% year-over-year during the quarter to $103.7 million. Marketplace revenue in the fourth fell 4.0% to $73.5 million. Services revenue soared 102.% to $30.2 million. Annual active buyers were down 10%. However, annual spend per buyer increased 9% to $302 and the marketplace take rate for the quarter was up 20 basis points to 27.6%.

Non-GAAP gross margin was down 60 basis points from a year ago to 84.0%. Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter was $20.7 million, compared to $16.1 million a year ago. Free cash flow was $29.6 million, compared to $27.4 million a year ago.

EPS was reported at $0.64 vs. $0.68 consensus and $0.60 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Fiverr (NYSE:FVRR) sees Q1 revenue of $103.5 million to 108.5 million (midpoint $106.0 million) vs. $102.1 million consensus, and full-year revenue of $422 million to $438 million (midpoint $430 million) vs. $418.6 million consensus. "Our ability to deliver growth, while showing continued discipline in driving profitability, demonstrates our steady progress towards achieving our Adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow three-year targets," stated CFO Ofer Katz. "We will continue to execute a thoughtful capital allocation strategy, balancing the need for growth as well as returning capital to shareholders. The guidance we gave for 2025 reflects our optimism as we expect to maintain momentum in Services revenue, continue upmarket investments, and lead in AI innovation," added Katz.

Shares of Fiverr International (FVRR) shot up 6.5% in premarket trading on Wednesday.

data.title: Southwest pact boosts Elliott max exposure to 19.9% from 14.9%
data.description:
Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) entered into an amendment to the cooperation agreement with Elliott Investment Management that increases the maximum aggregate economic exposure that the Elliott parties may acquire during the term of the pact to 19.9% from 14.9%, a filing showed on Wednesday.

Separately, on February 18, Ryan Green, Executive Vice President & Chief Transformation Officer of the company, informed Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) of his decision to step down from his position, effective April 1, 2025.

Earlier this week, the company said it would slash about 15% of its corporate jobs or 1,750 people in a cost-saving measure, after announcing a couple of decisions in January, including reducing pilot head count at a couple of bases and pause on hiring.

Southwest last year settled its boardroom feud with Elliott in a deal that allowed CEO Bob Jordan to retain his job by making bigger board-level concessions.

data.title: Travel+Leisure beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Travel+Leisure press release (NYSE:TNL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.72 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $971M (+3.9% Y/Y) beats by $12.13M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $252 million.
  • Repurchased $70 million of common stock during the fourth quarter.
  • Outlook: Full Year 2025 Adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $955 million to $985 million and first quarter 2025 Adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $195 million to $205 million
  • The Company will recommend increasing first quarter 2025 dividend to $0.56 per share for approval by the Board of Directors.
  • Outlook The Company is providing guidance for the 2025 full year: Adjusted EBITDA to range from $955 million to $985 million.
  • Gross VOI sales of $2.4 billion to $2.5 billion
  • VPG of $3,050 to $3,150.
  • Travel and Membership Adjusted EBITDA growth of flat to up 2%.
  • The Company is providing guidance for the first quarter 2025:
  • Adjusted EBITDA to range from $195 million to $205 million.
  • Gross VOI sales of $495 million to $515 million.
  • VPG of $3,150 to $3,250.

data.title: Loblaw looks to invest $10B in Canada over next five years
data.description:
• Loblaw (TSX:L:CA) said it will invest $2.2 billion into the Canadian economy in 2025, as it looks to improve access to more affordable food and healthcare services for Canadians.
  • The firm also expects to create an anticipated 8,000 jobs in communities across the country.
  • Loblaw (TSX:L:CA) said it anticipates similar levels of investment over the next five years and expects to invest more than $10 billion by 2030.
  • The investment plan includes: 80 new stores under the No Frills, Maxi, Shoppers Drug Mart, Pharmaprix and T&T banners, including approximately 50 hard discount stores.
  • It also includes the renovation of more than 300 grocery and pharmacy locations, including adding approximately 100 new Shoppers Drug Mart pharmacy care clinics, and the development of its modernized supply chain.

data.title: Parsons misses Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Parsons press release (NYSE:PSN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.78 misses by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $1.73B (+16.1% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Organic revenue growth of 14%; eighth consecutive quarter of double-digit organic growth.
  • Record adjusted EBITDA of $147 million increases 14%.
  • Cash flow from operations of $127 million.
  • Record contract awards increase 34% year-over-year to $1.7 billion.
  • Establishing fiscal year 2025 guidance and reiterating long-term growth targets.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Guidance

    The table below summarizes the company's fiscal year 2025 guidance.

    Fiscal Year2025 Guidance
    Revenue $7.0 billion - $7.5 billion vs. consensus of $7.34B
    Adjusted EBITDA including non-controlling interest $640 million - $710 million
    Cash Flow from Operating Activities $420 million - $480 million

    Net income guidance is not presented as the company believes volatility associated with interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization and other matters affecting net income, including but not limited to one-time and nonrecurring events and the impact of M&A, will preclude the company from providing, with reasonable certainty, net income guidance for fiscal year 2025.

    Reiterating Long-term Growth Targets

    The table below summarizes the company's long-term growth targets.

    Long-term Growth Targets Highlights
    Organic Revenue Growth Mid- single-digit or better organic growth Growth is off a revenue base that is $1.3 billion higher than fiscal year 2023
    Total Revenue Growth Mid- single-digit or better organic growth + M&A Growth is off a revenue base that is $1.3 billion higher than fiscal year 2023
    Adjusted EBITDA Margin Expansion Average 20 - 30 basis pointsper year Continual margin improvement opportunity. Adjusted EBITDA expansion also off a higher revenue base
    Free Cash Flow Conversion >100% Robust free cash flow generation to fund future organic and inorganic investment opportunities

data.title: FREYR Battery changes name to T1 Energy
data.description:
• FREYR Battery (NYSE:FREY) has renamed itself as T1 Energy in a strategic rebranding that also includes a new corporate logo, a fresh visual identity, and a forthcoming ticker symbol change to “TE” on the NYSE.
  • The T1 brand represents the company’s commitment to becoming a vertically integrated U.S. solar + battery storage leader from its new corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas, the company said in a news release.
  • In connection with the change, the company expects that effective as of the open of business on March 3, 2025, its common stock and warrants, which trade on the NYSE, will cease trading under the ticker symbols “FREY” and “FREY WS”, respectively, and commence trading under the ticker symbols “TE” and “TE WS”.
  • FREY +0.96% premarket to $2.09
  • Source: Press Release
